steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "The poem shouldn't be regarded as being simply a product of Brooks's ______ the breadth of the poet's influences..." The blank connects "surroundings" to the participial phrase "considering the breadth..." The word "though" is a parenthetical interrupter that signals a contrast. A parenthetical interrupter mid-sentence must be set off by commas on both sides, and the participial phrase must stay attached to the main clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — surroundings, though, considering: "though" is correctly enclosed by commas on both sides, and "considering" begins the participial phrase attached to the main clause. ✓
  • Choice B — surroundings, though. Considering: the period after "though" creates a sentence fragment — "Considering the breadth..." has no independent clause. ✗
  • Choice C — surroundings; though, considering: a semicolon before "though" is incorrect because "though, considering the breadth..." is not an independent clause and cannot follow a semicolon. ✗
  • Choice D — surroundings, though; considering: a semicolon after "though" is incorrect; "considering the breadth..." is a participial phrase, not an independent clause. ✗
That matches Choice A — surroundings, though, considering.
